title: Cherry Tomato Pasta with Crispy Chickpeas
ingredients: ½ cup extra virgin olive oil
1 can chickpeas, drained and patted dry
1 lb linguine or other long cut pasta
1 large bunch fresh basil, roughly chopped
2 balls fresh burrata cheese, at room temperature
instructions: Heat a large, high-sided skillet over medium heat.
Add the olive oil, chickpeas, and rosemary, and cook, stirring occasionally until the chickpeas are crisped all over, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the lemon zest and cook another minute until fried.
Remove the chickpeas, rosemary, and most of the lemon from the oil to a paper towel-lined plate and season with salt and pepper.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and add the pasta.
Cook the pasta according to package directions until al dente.
Just before draining, remove 1 cup of the pasta cooking water and then drain the pasta.
Place the skillet with the rosemary-infused oil over high heat.
When the oil shimmers, add the tomatoes, garlic, and a pinch each of salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Cook until the tomatoes begin to pop, about 4-5 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and add the vinegar, preserves or honey, and a splash of the pasta cooking water (3-4 tablespoons).
Simmer 5-10 minutes until the sauce reduces and is jammy.
Add butter to the sauce if desired to enhance creaminess.
Add the pasta, kale, and basil to the skillet, tossing to combine.
Crumble in the fried rosemary, reserving 1 sprig for topping.
Thin the sauce with additional pasta cooking water if needed.
Divide the pasta among plates and top with burrata cheese, crisp chickpeas, crumbled rosemary, and lemon.
notes: Some of the chickpeas will pop while frying, which is normal.
If using the optional butter, it enhances the creaminess of the sauce.
This recipe is from Half-Baked Harvest Cookbook.
Reserve 1 rosemary sprig for garnish before crumbling the rest into the pasta.
Serve the burrata cheese at room temperature for best flavor and texture.
